zh: “There’s something wrong with + (名词/动名词)”用来表示某物不正常运作或有问题。这个结构可以在正式和非正式场合中使用，是引起对问题关注的一种有效方式。 要使用这个结构，你需要在“there’s something wrong with”之后加上名词或动名词。在很多情况下，你的句子在这个名词或动名词之后就可以结束。 例如：There’s something wron
examples: Alice 和 Tom 正在讨论他们的打印机问题。
例如：There’s something wrong with them.
然而，你也可以添加更多信息来详细说明问题。例如：“There’s something wrong with them not talking to each other at all.”
You’d better… 的場景對話
John 和 Sarah 正在討論期末考試。
John: I’m really worried about the math exam tomorrow.
Don’t ever… 的場景對話
在學校操場上，Tom 和 Mike 正在談論他們的比賽計劃。
Mike: Thanks, Tom. I’ll try to stay positive and give it my best shot.
You seem… 的場景對話
Jessica 和 David 正在聊天。
Jessica: Hey David, you seem tired today. Did you stay up late last night?
Please make sure that… 的場景對話
在辦公室裡， Manager 和 Assistant 正在討論即將到來的項目。
Manager: We have a tight deadline for this project. Please make sure that everyone is aware of their tasks.
as…as possible… 的場景對話
Anna 和 Ben 正在討論他們的學期報告。
Anna: This report is really important for our final grade. We need to make sure it’s perfect.
Have you ever… 的場景對話
Alice 和 John 正在聊旅行經歷。
John: No, I haven’t, but I’ve always wanted to go. How about you?
There’s no way… 的場景對話
Lisa 和 Tom 正在討論即將到來的考試。
Lisa: I’m really worried about the math exam tomorrow.
How come… 的場景對話
Jane 和 Tom 正在討論昨天的會議。
Jane: How come you didn’t attend the meeting yesterday? It was really important.
Let me…  的場景對話
Emma 和 Jack 正在準備一個小組項目。
Emma: I’m almost done with my part of the project. How about you, Jack?

pattern: What if + 主詞 + 動詞
examples: What if… 的場景對話
Mary 和 John 正在討論即將舉辦的野餐。
Mary: I’m excited about our picnic tomorrow. Do you think everything is ready?
What do you mean by… 的場景對話
在辦公室裡，Alice 和她的同事 Tom 正在討論一個項目報告。
What do you mean by… 的句型解釋和用法

pattern: On one hand…but on the other hand
zh: “On one hand…but on the other hand”
examples: On one hand…but on the other hand 的場景對話
Sarah 和 Tom 正在討論是否應該換工作。
On one hand…but on the other hand 的句型解釋和用法
I’m wondering if… 的場景對話
在辦公室裡，Emily 和她的同事 Michael 正在討論一個新項目。
Emily: Hey Michael, I’m wondering if you have any updates on the new project timeline.
I’ve been…… 的場景對話
在咖啡店裡，Anna 和 Lisa 正在聊天。
Anna: You look tired, Lisa. Is everything okay?
not only…,but also…… 的場景對話
Emily 和 Jane 正在討論他們共同認識的新朋友 Mark。
Emily: Have you met Mark? He’s quite an interesting person.
No matter how/what/where… 的場景對話
John 和 Lisa 正在討論即將到來的考試。
John: I’m really worried about the math exam tomorrow. It’s so difficult.
I’m here to… 的場景對話
Michael 正在與接待員對話。
Michael: Hi, I’m here to see Mr. Johnson about the new project.

pattern: What’s the matter with + 名詞/動名詞
zh: “What’s the matter with + 名詞/動名詞”
examples: What’s the matter with… 的場景對話
Nina: You look worried, Matt. What’s the matter with you?
Matt: I’ve had a severe headache since last night. It’s not getting any better.
Once you… 的場景對話
Anna 和 Lisa 正在討論如何適應新的學期。
Anna: I’m feeling a bit overwhelmed with all the new classes and schedules.
The more… the more… 的場景對話
Emma 和 Jack 正在討論他們的健身進展。
Emma: I’ve noticed something interesting about my workouts.

pattern: I can hardly believe that+主詞+動詞
zh: “I can hardly believe that+主詞+動詞”
examples: I can hardly believe that… 的場景對話
Jenny 和 Michael 正在談論一位共同朋友的驚人消息。
Jenny: I can hardly believe that Sarah is moving to Paris next month.
I’ve heard that… 的場景對話
在午餐時間，Alice 和 Bob 正在討論最近聽到的消息。
Bob: Really? That’s exciting news. Do you know when they plan to start?

pattern: it takes somebody + 多少時間 + to
zh: “it takes somebody + 多少時間 + to”
examples: it takes somebody + 多少時間… 的場景對話
在公司休息室裡，Rachel 和 Tom 正在討論他們的通勤時間。
Rachel: I can’t believe it takes me an hour and a half to get to work every day.
Are you sure… 的場景對話
Lisa 和 Mark 正在討論即將提交的銷售報告。
Lisa: I’ve included all the data from the last quarter. I think it looks comprehensive.

pattern: It looks like + 名詞/主詞+動詞
zh: “It looks like + 名詞/主詞+動詞”
examples: It looks like… 的場景對話
Alice 和 Bob 正在觀察窗外的天氣。
Bob: Yes, the sky is quite dark. It looks like a storm is coming.
I’m calling to… 的場景對話
在辦公室，Jennifer 正在撥打電話給客戶服務中心。
Jennifer: Hello, I’m calling to inquire about the status of my order placed last week.

pattern: I had a hard time + 動詞-ing
zh: “ I had a hard time + 動詞-ing”
examples: I had a hard time… 的場景對話
Eva 和 Tom 正在讨论他们最近的工作挑战。
Eva: I had a hard time understanding the new policy changes. Did you check the latest updates?
Why didn’t you… 的場景對話
在學校的圖書館裡，Lily 和 Max 正在討論一個即將到來的考試。
Lily: Max, why didn’t you attend the study group yesterday? We covered a lot of important topics.

pattern: I’m sorry to + 動詞, but…
examples: I’m sorry to, but… 的場景對話
Sarah: Of course, Tom.
I’m sorry to, but… 的句型解釋和用法

pattern: If I were you, I would + 動詞
zh: “If I were you, I would + 動詞”
examples: If I were you, I would 的場景對話
Emily 和 Jack 正在討論未來的職業規劃。
Emily: I’m not sure what I should do next in my career. I have a few options, but I’m really confused.

pattern: What would you do if + 主詞 + 過去式動詞
zh: “What would you do if + 主詞 + 過去式動詞”
examples: What would you do if 的場景對話
Tom 和 Lucy 在討論如何應對不同的情況。
Lucy: Oh, that’s a tough one. If my flight was canceled, I would probably try to rebook it for another time. What about you, Tom?

pattern: I’m good at + 名詞/動詞-ing
examples: I’m good at 的場景對話
Mark: Not yet, Tom. I’m trying to figure out what I’m good at.
Mark: Yeah, I’m considering that. But I’m also good at painting, so maybe I’ll join the art club too.
I used to 的場景對話
Alice 和 Ben 正在回憶他們的兒時時光。
Ben: I used to go to a summer camp near the lake. We would swim every day. How times have changed!
It’s not that …but… 的場景對話
Emily 和 Jack 正在討論即將到來的學生活動。
Emily: I know everyone is excited about the upcoming campus festival, but we need to consider our budget carefully.
By the way 的場景對話
Grace 和 Henry 正在討論他們即將到來的演講準備。
Grace: I think I’ve covered all the major points for the presentation on environmental sustainability.
It’s no use 的場景對話
Natalie 和 Eric 正在為即將到來的考試做準備。
Natalie: I’ve been trying to understand this chapter for hours, but it’s just too complex.
Do you like 的場景對話
Julia 和 Tom 正在談論他們的興趣。
Julia: So, Tom, I’ve been getting into baking recently. Do you like baking?
the main point is that 的場景對話
在學院的討論會上，Linda 和 Tom 正在討論一個關於氣候變遷的熱門話題。
Linda: Exactly.
Do you want me to 的場景對話
Sarah 和 Mark 正在計劃一個慈善活動。
Sarah: We have a lot to cover before the event next weekend.
I have no idea 的場景對話
Anna 和 Liam 正在討論即將到來的考試。
Anna: Have you figured out what the professor expects for the final question on the exam?
I want to 的場景對話
Olivia 和 Ethan 正在討論他們的暑期計畫。
Olivia: I’ve been thinking a lot about what I want to do this summer.
What do you think about 的場景對話
Ella 和 Ben 正在討論即將到來的活動。
Ella: What do you think about the theme for this year’s spring festival?
I’m trying to 的場景對話
Sophie 和 James 正在談論他們的新年決心。
Sophie: I’ve been thinking a lot about my goals for this year. I’m trying to read more books.
It may surprise you but 的場景對話
Sophie 和 Evan 正在討論他們對於書籍的偏好。
Sophie: I’ve always been a huge fan of classic literature.
Emily 和 Tom 正在為即將到來的期末考試做準備。
Emily: Exactly, and no matter how much I study, I can’t help doubting if it’s enough.
例如，當有人在正式場合說了一些有趣的話，你忍不住笑或咯咯地笑。然後你會說 I’m sorry, but I can’t help laughing as the way he said it was hilarious. （對不起，但我忍不住笑，因為他說的方式太有趣了。）

pattern: I don’t know what to do with
zh: “I don’t know what to do with”
examples: I don’t know what to do with 的場景對話
Hannah 和 Lucas 正在討論他們的團隊專案。
Hannah: We’ve collected so much data, I don’t know what to do with it.
I’m working on 的場景對話
在校園的一角，Emma 和 Ryan 正在午餐时间讨论他们的项目。
Emma: How’s your part of the project going?
I feel like 的場景對話
Zoe 和 Alex 正享受着周末的悠闲时光。
Zoe: This weekend is just perfect. I feel like going for a walk by the beach.

pattern: No wonder + 主詞 + 動詞
examples: No wonder 的場景對話
在圖書館外的一個安靜角落，Lena 和 Marco 正在討論他們的最新科學項目。
Lena: Did you hear about the new study on climate change?

pattern: I should have + 過去分詞
examples: 在咖啡館裡，Leo 和 Claire 正在談論他們昨天錯過的音樂會。
Leo: I still can’t believe we missed the concert last night.
Claire: I know, I should have set an alarm. I completely forgot about it until it was too late.
I gotta 的場景對話
在城市的一個繁忙街角，Max 和 Olivia 正在享受他們的午後時光。
Max: This coffee is really good. I’m glad we decided to meet up today.
How often do you 的場景對話
在健身房休息區，Mark 和 Lucy 正在談論他們的運動習慣。
Mark: I’ve been trying to make exercise a regular part of my routine. How often do you come to the gym?
How much does it cost 的場景對話
在一家新開咖啡廳裡，Jake 和 Sara 正在瀏覽菜單。
Jake: This place looks amazing. What are you thinking of ordering?
I’m not sure if 的場景對話
在學校的圖書館裡，Lily 和 Ethan 正在討論他們的科學項目。
Lily: We need to decide on our project topic by tomorrow. I was thinking about something related to renewable energy.
Do/Would you mind if 的場景對話
Anna 和 Ben 正在一個安靜的地方讀書。
Anna: This spot looks perfect. Do you mind if I open the window a bit? It’s a bit stuffy in here.
I’m thinking of 的場景對話
Elena 和Tom，正在咖啡館裡談論他們的未來計劃。
Elena: I’ve been feeling a bit stuck lately. I’m thinking of taking a break and going on a long vacation.
I have to 的場景對話
在一個家庭聚會上，Michael 和他的姐姐 Emily 正在討論他們的下周計劃。
Michael: I can’t believe how quickly next week is approaching. I have so much to do.
It’s my fault for 的場景對話
在一次團隊會議後，Elena 和 Tom 討論了一個項目上出現的錯誤。
Elena: I’ve reviewed the project report, and I noticed there are several inaccuracies.
Sarah 和 Daniel，正在談論即將到來的考試。
Sarah: I’m so nervous about the math exam next week. I haven’t started studying yet.
Daniel: Don’t worry, I’ll help you prepare.
兩位朋友，Max 和 Emma，正在討論即將到來的音樂節。
Max: Have you seen the weather forecast for this weekend? The music festival we’ve been looking forward to might be rained out.
Emma: Really? What are the chances of that happening? I thought it was supposed to be sunny.
在家中的一個晚上，Ella 和 Max 正在討論他們的晚飯。
Ella: Look at the clock, Max. It’s already 8 PM.
Max: Oh, you’re right. It’s time to start preparing dinner, isn’t it?
在家庭聚會的晚上，Emma 和她的兄弟 Alex 正準備玩一個遊戲。
Emma: Okay, we finished setting up the game. Who’s going first?
Alex: Since I went first last time, it’s your turn to start the game tonight.
兩位好友，Liam 和 Sophie，正在計劃他們的週末活動。
Liam: So, what do you feel like doing this weekend? There’s a new horror movie playing at the cinema.
Sophie: Hmm, I’d rather stay home than go out to watch a movie.
某天下午，Anna 在圖書館裡遇到了她的朋友 Eric。Eric 剛好幫忙找到了一本她一直尋找的書。
Anna: Oh, Eric! You found it! I’ve been looking for this book for weeks.
Eric: Yeah, I remembered you mentioned it last time we met. I saw it by chance when I was browsing through the library shelves.
David 剛從朋友那裡收到了聚會邀請。
David: Hey, Lisa! I just got your invitation for the party next week.
Lisa: Yes, I’m so excited about it! Are you coming?
David 剛從英國搬到台灣，和他當地的朋友Eva在一家咖啡館相遇，正談論他還不適應當地環境。
David: The weather here is so hot and humid. I’m not used to this kind of climate at all.
Eva: Yeah, it can take some time to adjust. Maybe try wearing lighter clothes?
在一個周末的咖啡廳場景中，Linda 和 Alex 正在討論他們的興趣和近期的願望。
Linda: I heard there’s a new art exhibition in town. Have you seen it yet?
Alex: No, I haven’t, but I’m dying to go! I’ve always been a fan of modern art.
Jenny: Oh, they’re for Mark’s surprise birthday party this weekend. We’re planning to decorate the living room and make it really special.
Tom：嘿，Jenny，我昨天看到你買了很多裝飾品。 它們是做什麼用的?Jenny：哦，這是 Mark 本週末的驚喜生日派對用的。 我們計劃裝飾客廳，讓它變得非常特別。
例如，我們可以問朋友“what are you doing that for?”來代替“why are you doing that?”。
